title: Elaeagnus iliensis
intro: Elaeagnus iliensis, also known as Ili shrub or Ili silverberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of western China, Kazakhstan, and Kyrgyzstan. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating a range of conditions, from digestive issues to respiratory problems. Bioactive constituents of Elaeagnus iliensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Elaeagnus iliensis is classified within the family Elaeagnaceae, commonly known as the silverberry or elephant apple family. This family is an order of flowering plants native to Asia and parts of North America. Elaeagnus iliensis, specifically, belongs to the genus Elaeagnus, which consists of approximately 70 species of shrubs and small trees. The genus Elaeagnus is further divided into two subgenera: Elaeagnus and Choanoxylon. Elaeagnus iliensis is often found in dry environments and is valued for its ornamental and medicinal properties.
uses: Elaeagnus iliensis is a plant that has been traditionally used in medicine in its native range. It is known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential medicinal uses. Elaeagnus iliensis has been studied for its ability to inhibit the growth of certain pathogens and reduce inflammation in animal models. The plant's leaves, stems, and roots are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and other herbal remedies. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential benefits of Elaeagnus iliensis.
benefits: Elaeagnus iliensis, also known as the Tibetan silverberry, has been found to have various health benefits due to its rich content of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds. The plant's leaves, bark, and seeds contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Research suggests that Elaeagnus iliensis may help to protect against oxidative stress, cell damage, and other diseases. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-cancer, and anti-diabetic activities. Further studies are needed to fully understand the health benefits and potential applications of Elaeagnus iliensis.
properties: Elaeagnus iliensis is a plant species known to have various therapeutic properties. Elaeagnus iliensis contains compounds that may have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. These compounds have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids present in Elaeagnus iliensis may also have potential in protecting against cardiovascular diseases and neurodegenerative disorders.
